data.description:
# Tier-3: CI-scheduled enrichment drain (GitHub Actions)

## Context
Follow-on to the cross-ticket overlap epic (only-crave-art). Because the store is git-backed and already pushed to a remote, a scheduled GitHub Action (`on: schedule:`) can run `rebar enrich --drain` against the pushed store — zero LOCAL setup, the Renovate/Dependabot model. rebar already runs Actions for ticket→Jira sync, so the harness exists.

## Why deferred (product-owner decision)
We want to confirm the system works WITHOUT the CI job before implementing CI as a primary mechanism, because MANY CLIENTS WILL NOT HAVE dedicated CI configured. Tier 1 (opportunistic detached drain) must carry the feature on its own first.

## Known caveats to design around
- GitHub cron has a 5-min floor and 5-30+ min delays under load (best-effort cadence, not an SLA).
- Scheduled workflows auto-disable after ~60 days of repo inactivity.
- Pair `schedule:` with `workflow_dispatch:`; use a GitHub App token / OIDC (the bare GITHUB_TOKEN can't update workflows). The API key must be a CI secret.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A committed `.github/workflows/` drain runs `rebar enrich --drain` on a schedule and pushes results back.
- [ ] Manually triggerable via `workflow_dispatch`.
- [ ] Documented as best-effort (imprecise cadence, inactivity auto-disable) and optional — Tier 1 remains the primary mechanism.
data.title: Tier-3: CI-scheduled enrichment drain (GitHub Actions)
